assigned_task: Verify worker claim gate role enforcement for an engineering task

answers.What already exists?: Agentic Nexus policy, machine-readable schemas, worker registry, dashboard summary, and worker claim gate all exist under .agentic_nexus.
answers.What has already been started?: Diagnostic layer, authorisation policy, schema package, validation report, task queue, claim gate, completion gate, approval store, dashboard feed, worker registry, and worker heartbeat have all been started and validated.
answers.Why was it created?: To stop agents receiving work without context, scope boundaries, evidence, and approval checks, and to restrict claims to registered, available, role-appropriate workers.
answers.What problem was it meant to solve?: Uncontrolled or unclear agent execution across the Unite-Group Nexus ecosystem.
answers.What friction is it trying to reduce?: Repeated explanations, duplicated work, weak evidence, unsafe autonomous actions, and unregistered workers claiming tasks.
answers.What is missing?: End-to-end role enforcement has to be verified through a fixture task assigned to a research worker that should be refused.
answers.What is duplicated?: No duplicate runtime validator or worker registry currently exists in this folder.
answers.What is unclear?: Worker integration for the three physical computers still needs end-to-end verification.
answers.What benefits Unite-Group Nexus as a who: Creates a fail-closed gate before local workers execute tasks, including a registry check that blocks unregistered and unavailable workers.
answers.What is the smallest useful next action?: Run worker_claim_task.py with a research worker for an engineering task and verify it is refused for role mismatch.

diagnostic_gate.next_action: Validate session and then run worker_claim_task.py for task_worker_registry_wrong_role_001 with a research worker.

requested_actions: verify claim gate role enforcement for an engineering task from verified evidence
